paper: ParetOrch: Cost-Quality Pareto Optimization for Multi-Agent LLM Topology Selection

methodology_skeptic.key_concern: 84 tasks underpowered; potential train-test contamination in quality predictor; simulated costs not validated

novelty_assessor.key_concern: Routing mechanism is logistic regression + argmin (limited technical novelty); DIT features inherited not introduced; 3 topologies may be too narrow

clarity_reviewer.key_concern: Placeholder figures; 5 contributions should be 3; self-assessment metadata leaks; no broader impact statement

domain_expert.key_concern: No real-world deployment validation; DIT annotation scalability unaddressed; no latency analysis

ethics_reproducibility.key_concern: Repository URL placeholder; DIT annotation process opaque; task provenance unclear; missing training details

threshold_details.notes: 2 reviewers at 5 (borderline reject), 2 at 6 (borderline accept), 1 at 7 (accept). Quality aggregate 5.8 < 6.0 threshold. No confirmed fatal flaw but train/test contamination risk is near-fatal.

verdict: BORDERLINE REJECT -- Revise and resubmit. Paper has genuine contribution (topology-level routing framing, DIT crossover insight, composability argument) but critical gaps in methodology (potential train-test contamination, simulated costs), reproducibility (placeholder repo, opaque DIT annotation), and presentation (missing figures, metadata leaks). Estimated 1-2 weeks of targeted revision to reach acceptance threshold.

remaining_fixes.detail: Clarify evaluation protocol for logistic regression. If trained on all 84 tasks, implement k-fold CV and re-report.

remaining_fixes.issue: Anonymous repository with code and data
remaining_fixes.detail: Create anonymous repo with: 84 task descriptions + DIT annotations, 2016 run outputs, routing code, training code, reproduction scripts.
Document: who annotated (authors/crowd/LLM), guidelines, inter-annotator agreement (kappa/alpha), validation process.
Replace [FIGURE] placeholders with actual visualizations: Fig 1 (system diagram), Fig 2 (cost by tier), Fig 3 (Pareto frontier).

remaining_fixes.issue: Cost model validation against real API billing
remaining_fixes.detail: Validate simulated costs vs actual API charges for a task subset, or conduct robustness analysis with +/-20% cost noise.
Add oracle routing row to Table 1 showing minimum cost at 100% accuracy across available topologies.
Merge: (1) Topology Pareto routing + frontier analysis, (2) DIT routing + crossover insight, (3) Empirical evaluation.
Add case study on 50-100 tasks from real agent deployment or public benchmark (GAIA, SWE-bench).

remaining_fixes.issue: DIT extraction automation and error propagation
remaining_fixes.detail: Train DIT classifier, report extraction accuracy, measure downstream routing impact of estimation errors.

remaining_fixes.issue: Strip self-assessment metadata from all sections
remaining_fixes.detail: Remove Self-Assessment, Known Weaknesses, and Revision Notes blocks before LaTeX compilation.
Report: regularization type/strength, optimizer, convergence criteria, Brier score, ECE calibration.
Add NeurIPS-required broader impact: cost democratization, quality shortcut risks, environmental considerations.
Report wall-clock latency per topology per difficulty tier.

remaining_fixes.issue: Cost model stratified by task category
remaining_fixes.detail: Test overhead ratio independence from task type (coding vs summarization etc).

remaining_fixes.issue: Sharpen DAAO distinction with concrete example
remaining_fixes.detail: Provide concrete task where operator-level and topology-level routing diverge.
Narrow CIs and strengthen marginal claims (e.g. 2.7pp gap between ParetOrch and DIT Accuracy-Only).
Complete and include the NeurIPS reproducibility checklist.
